title: Polymorphic forms and amorphous solid dispersion of selexipag
abstract: The present disclosure relates to crystalline forms of selexipag and their processes for preparation. The present disclosure also relates to an amorphous solid dispersion of selexipag and its processes for their preparation as well as premix of crystalline selexipag and their process.
claims: Claims We claim: 1 . Crystalline selexipag Form V. 2. Crystalline selexipag Form V of claim 1 characterized by a powder X-ray diffraction pattern with substantial peaks at 2Î values of 5.93, 1 1 .14, 19.73, 19.96, 20.98, 21 .15, 22.21 , and 23.34 Â± 0.2 s as shown in Figure 2. 3. A process for the preparation of selexipag Form V comprising the steps of: a) dissolving selexipag in anisole; b) optionally seeding with selexipag Form V; c) optionally adding an anti-solvent; and d) isolating selexipag Form V. 4. The process according to claims 3, wherein the anti-solvent is selected from ether solvents such as without limitation, diethyl ether, diisopropylether, methyl tert-butyl ether and the hydrocarbon solvents is selected from hexane, cyclohexane, methyl cyclohexane, heptane and pentane and mixtures thereof. 5. Crystalline selexipag Form VII-1 . 6. Crystalline selexipag Form VII-1 of claim 5characterized by a powder X-ray diffraction pattern with substantial peaks at 2Î values of 6.29, 18.79, 19.31 , 23.3, 23.7, 24.5, and 25.0 Â± 0.2 s as shown in Figure 5. 7. A process for the preparation of crystalline Form VII-1 of selexipag comprising the steps of: a) dissolving selexipag in toluene; b) seeding with selexipag Form VI; and c) isolating selexipag Form VII-1 . 8. Crystalline selexipag Form IV. 9. Crystalline selexipag Form IV of claim 8 characterized by a powder X-ray diffraction pattern with substantial peaks at 2Î values of 5.73, 17.68, 18.32, 19.16, 19.45, 20.59, 20.77, and 23.13 Â± 0.2 s as shown in Figure 1 . 10. A process for the preparation of selexipag Form IV comprising the steps of: a) dissolving selexipag in o-xylene; b) optionally seeding with selexipag Form IV; and c) isolating selexipag Form IV. 1 1 . Crystalline selexipag Form VI. 12. Crystalline selexipag Form VI of claim 1 1 characterized by a powder X-ray diffraction pattern with substantial peaks at 2Î values of 6.38, 6.94, 10.69, 13.74, 18.44, 18.97, 19.43, 21 .32, 23.35, 24.01 , 24.71 , 25.23, and 26.25 Â± 0.2 s as shown in Figure 3. 13. A process for the preparation of selexipag Form VI comprising the steps of: a) dissolving selexipag in chlorobenzene; b) optionally seeding with selexipag Form VI; and c) isolating selexipag Form VI. 14. The amorphous solid dispersion of selexipag and a pharmaceutically acceptable carrier prepared by hot-melt extrusion. 15. The solid dispersion of claim 14, wherein the selexipag is selected from crystalline selexipag Form IV, V, VI, VII-1 and mixtures thereof. 16. The solid dispersion of claim 14, wherein the pharmaceutically acceptable carrier is selected from the group consisting of Plasdone S-630, povidone K-30, and mixtures thereof.
